content:
# Dammam Dome

**Summary**: A large carbonate dome structure in the Eastern Province of Saudi Arabia.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2018-hanif|Hanif, 2018]] studied the dammam-dome to assess karst and fault risks.

- [[2023-edigbue|Edigbue, 2023]] used as a case study to demonstrate the effectiveness of integrated geological and geophysical mapping.

- [[2023-alshaibani|AlShaibani, 2023]] investigated the deformation zone within the Dammam Dome.

## Geological and Structural Setting

- [[2018-hanif|Hanif, 2018]] doming mechanism and dissolution

- [[2023-edigbue|Edigbue, 2023]] used as a case study to demonstrate the effectiveness of integrated geological and geophysical mapping.

- [[2023-alshaibani|AlShaibani, 2023]] investigated the deformation zone within the Dammam Dome.

## Formations Present

- [[2018-hanif|Hanif, 2018]] Rus Formation, Umm Er Radhuma Formation

## Key Findings From This Location

- The dome is characterized by fractured carbonates with joints, karst features, and small-scale reverse faults. ([[2018-hanif|Hanif, 2018, p. 17]])

- The inversion results provided valuable information regarding the location, depth, and extent of subsurface structures within the dome. ([[2023-edigbue|Edigbue, 2023, p. 117]])

- The dome covers an area of about 500 km2 and has an oval-shaped structure. ([[2023-alshaibani|AlShaibani, 2023, p. 16]])
